aquacroptools 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacroptools

Write AquaCrop MAN files in batch

Source: R/write_man_batch.R
write_man_batch.Rd

Generate AquaCrop MAN files for multiple stations in a batch.

write_man_batch(station_name = NULL, params = NULL, verbose = TRUE, ...)

Arguments

station_name

Character vector or NULL. Names of stations to process:

  • If NULL, all stations are automatically discovered from .CLI files in the CLIMATE/ directory.

  • If a vector, only the specified stations will be processed; all must have corresponding climate files.

params

Either:

  • A single named list of management parameters (var_02 to var_21), applied to all stations, or

  • A list of named lists (one per station), each containing management parameters. The length of the list must match the number of stations. Set to NULL or list() to use all default values for all stations. See ManData documentation for further details.

verbose

Logical. If TRUE (default), prints progress messages. If FALSE, runs silently.

...

Additional arguments passed to write_man, such as:

  • path: Output directory path (default: "man/")

  • eol: End-of-line type (default: "windows")

  • climate_path: Path to climate file directory (default: "CLIMATE/")

  • base_path: Base absolute path (default: current working directory)

Value

Invisibly returns NULL. The main effect is writing MAN files to the specified directory.

Details

The function validates that all specified stations have corresponding climate files.

A single list of parameters will be automatically applied to all stations.

Valid parameters include var_02 through var_21. See ManData documentation for further details on parameter descriptions and valid ranges.

Use this function when you need to create MAN files for multiple stations at once.

See also

write_man for single management file generation, write_prm_batch for batch PRM file generation, ManData for complete parameter reference

Other batch operations: write_prm_batch()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Example 1: Multiple stations, same management parameters
base_params <- list(var_03 = 20, var_04 = 60, var_05 = 50)
stations <- c("grid_001", "grid_002")
write_man_batch(
  station_name = stations,
  params = base_params
)

# Example 2: Multiple stations, different management parameters
params_list <- list(
  list(var_03 = 0, var_04 = 0, var_05 = 30), # Low fertility, no mulch
  list(var_03 = 60, var_04 = 80, var_05 = 70) # High fertility, heavy mulch
)
write_man_batch(
  station_name = stations,
  params = params_list,
  path = "MANAGEMENT/"
)

# Example 3: Auto-discover all stations, default parameters
write_man_batch(
  station_name = NULL,
  params = NULL,
  base_path = "/my/project/path",
  verbose = FALSE
)

# Example 4: Station-specific fertility gradient
# See ManData documentation for all available parameters
params_list <- list(
  list(var_05 = 30), # Station 1: low fertility
  list(var_05 = 60), # Station 2: medium fertility
  list(var_05 = 90) # Station 3: high fertility
)
write_man_batch(
  station_name = c("site_A", "site_B", "site_C"),
  params = params_list
)
} # }
